evidence.target_exercise_snippet: The table below shows the number of ice creams sold at different prices: P=€1.00→Q=500, €1.50→400, €2.00→300, €2.50→200, €3.00→100. a. Draw this as a graph with price on the vertical axis and quantity on the horizontal axis. b. Read from your graph: how many ice creams are sold at €1.75? c. A newspaper writes: 'Ice cream sales dropped by 50%.' Looking at the table, between which two prices could this have happened...

evidence.target_exercise_snippet: A wheat farmer sells wheat at the market price of €200 per ton. He cannot influence this price. a. Name four characteristics of a perfectly competitive market. For each, explain why the wheat market fits. b. The farmer considers charging €210 per ton. Explain why this won't work. c. Draw the farmer's individual price line. Label it P = GO = MO. d. Explain why for this farmer MO = price.

evidence.target_exercise_snippet: A pension of €1500/month. Inflation 4%, average wage growth 6%. Students compare three indexering regimes: waardevast, welvaartsvast, bevroren. a. The pension is waardevast (indexed to inflation). Calculate the new pension. b. The pension is welvaartsvast (indexed to wage growth). Calculate the new pension. c. The pension is bevroren (frozen). Calculate the real purchasing power loss in euros per month. d. Which i...

triage_records.paragraph_title: Real vs nominal: integration and application
triage_records.raw_flag: Compute real interest rate ≈ nominal − inflation and interpret purchasing-power effect on savings (concept-level + reken; partial overlap with H15 which is macro-framed)
triage_records.rationale: Raw flag explicitly references existing unit H15 and asks for scope verification rather than a new unit.

evidence.target_exercise_snippet: Integration lesson applying the real vs nominal distinction to interest rates, GDP growth, wages, and central bank policy. a. The nominal interest rate on a savings account is 5%. Inflation is 3%. Calculate the real interest rate (use: real ≈ nominal − inflation). If you save €10,000, how much more can you actually buy after one year? b. Country X's nominal GDP grew from €800 billion to €840 billion. The GDP defla...
